summary: Broad cleanup of function signatures across camera, fighter, game-mode, stage, item, lb, and baselib code. The author used Claude-generated scripts to infer types from asm, then applied legitimate findings: converting integer returns to bool or void, removing unused parameters, correcting parameter order/types, adding return types for item-spawning helpers, and syncing headers/struct callback fields. The decomp.dev bot reported +0.01% matched code, +292 bytes, two new full matches in gm_1601, and multiple unmatched-item improvements.
what_changed: Changed Camera_8002E158 and Camera_8002E234 from s32 returns to bool and updated camera.h prototypes; local accumulator variables were renamed/typed as bool ret and initialized with false/true.

Removed unused parameters from several functions and call sites, including Fighter_8006CDA4(Fighter*, s32), ftKb_SpecialN_800F5898(Fighter_GObj*), ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28(void), it_802A78B8(HSD_GObj*, Vec3*), it_802BAAE4(Item_GObj*, Vec3*), and fn_801CFAFC(..., HSD_GObj*).

Corrected function return types where asm/call behavior indicated no meaningful return, including fn_80168A6C to void, fn_80192E6C to void, fn_801967E0 to void, itColl_BounceOffVictim to void, it_802B1FC8 to void, it_802B22B8 to void, it_8028D390 to void, it_802B64FC to void, it_802B6A60 to void, it_802BBC38 to void, and it_80291CCC to void.
Corrected item factory/spawn helpers to return Item_GObj* where the created object was returned, including it_802EAF34, it_802996D0, it_802C519C, and the it_802B338C prototype.

Corrected argument types and order in game/stage callbacks: gm_801674C4 takes bool (*)(s32), fn_80169434 stores a bool (*)(s32), gm static struct field x1B8 became bool (*)(s32), grIceMt_801F96E0/grIceMt_801F9ACC take a Ground_GObj/HSD_GObj plus y, grKinokoRoute_80208480 and grShrineRoute_8020AD24 take s32 rather than bool, and grYorster_802022A4 takes a ground object argument.
Fixed call-site ordering for it_802BE9D8 in Ness yoyo creation from (facing_dir, gobj, pos, motion_id) to (gobj, facing_dir, pos, motion_id).

Updated library/baselib typing: lb_800138CC now takes HSD_GObjEvent and the related lb struct gained an HSD_GObjEvent field x18; baselib memory-card helpers such as fn_803AC258/fn_803B1338 now use CardState* instead of raw s32.
Replaced dummy stack arrays and solution preprocessor scaffolding with PAD_STACK in places such as fighter.c and ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.c.
Large formatting and cast-spacing cleanup accompanied the signature changes, especially in grshrineroute.c and lb_00F9.c.
smart_moves: Used asm-driven type inference as a broad audit tool, but the PR body explicitly notes the scripts produced false positives and false negatives, so the committed changes appear curated rather than blindly automated.
Synchronized C definitions, headers, and struct callback fields in the same PR, preventing later prototype drift across call sites.
Converted integer flag returns to bool only where the code was already operating on true/false semantics, such as Camera_8002E158/Camera_8002E234 and gm callback fields.
Removed fake unused parameters instead of preserving misleading signatures; call sites were then simplified, reducing temporary variables that existed only to feed bogus arguments.
Used PAD_STACK to retain matching stack layout after removing unused parameters or dummy local arrays, avoiding regression from cleaner source.
Promoted raw integer callback storage in gm_1601.static.h to a typed function pointer, making later decomp work less dependent on M2C_FIELD-style casts.
decomp_lessons: When a function only returns 0/1 and callers combine results with boolean logic, changing s32 to bool can improve both readability and matching, but headers must be updated simultaneously.
Unused parameters inferred by decompilation can be artifacts; verify against asm call setup before accepting them as real signature members.
If a function's return register is ignored or not meaningfully set, a void return may be the correct signature and can eliminate bogus return expressions.
Factory functions that call item creation routines often should return Item_GObj*; preserving that return type can expose object ownership patterns and help callers match.
Callback fields in structs should be given exact function-pointer types as soon as call signatures are known; raw s32/int storage hides useful type constraints.
Stack padding macros are preferable to fake unused variables or preprocessor solution blocks when the only remaining purpose is preserving stack shape.
Tool-assisted signature scans are useful for finding real mismatches across many subsystems, but they need manual filtering because the author reported substantial script noise.
naming_conventions: Use bool for true/false return values and locals, with true/false literals instead of 1/0 where source structure permits.
Use void for no-argument functions whose asm signature does not consume an argument, e.g. ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28(void) and it_80274EE8(void).
Use subsystem object pointer aliases in signatures rather than raw integers where known, such as Ground_GObj*/HSD_GObj*, Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, CardState*, and HSD_GObjEvent.
For callback fields and parameters, spell the function pointer type directly, e.g. bool (*fn)(s32) or bool (*arg4)(s32), instead of s32/int placeholders.
Use PAD_STACK(n) for intentional stack padding rather than local u8 arrays named underscore or volatile padding variables.
Keep address-comment prototype declarations in headers synchronized with the corresponding C definition.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Infer signatures by examining asm for argument setup, return register use, and callback invocation shape.
Remove unused call arguments when asm indicates the callee does not consume them; this can eliminate artificial temporaries and improve codegen.
Convert functions to void when no return value is semantically produced, avoiding forced casts like return (s32) fn(...).
Use bool returns for functions that set only 0/1 and are consumed through comparisons or bitwise/boolean accumulation.
Preserve stack layout with PAD_STACK after source simplification so matching does not depend on fake locals.
Correct parameter order when call-site asm contradicts earlier guesses, as with it_802BE9D8 taking the owner gobj before facing_dir.
Typed callback fields can remove M2C_FIELD raw stores and make indirect calls match with explicit parameters.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body says the type-inference scripts were Claude-written, uncommitted, and noisy, but useful for finding legitimate mismatches.
decomp.dev bot report for GALE01 showed matched code 63.10% (+0.01%, +292 bytes), with new full matches for gm_1601 fn_80168A6C and fn_80169444.
Bot also reported improvements in ftCo_8008DCE0, it_802BBC38, grIceMt_801F7728, it_802B64FC, ftCo_800BDB58, ftCo_800BE000, it_802996D0, it_802C519C, it_802EAF34, it_802B22B8, and it_802DC4BC.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue asm-inferred function signature fixes from PR 2413.
Audit remaining functions with s32 returns that only produce 0/1 for possible bool signatures.
Search for remaining fake unused parameters in item factory/spawn functions.
Search for remaining raw s32 callback fields that should be typed as function pointers.
Compare PR 2413 PAD_STACK replacements with later stack-padding conventions.
